# Autonomous heal action: `quarantine`-from-serving (the safe-default terminal that lets `escalate` be deleted) â€” #14132 sub

## Context

Child of #14132 (delete `escalate` â†’ 100% autonomous self-heal). The data-integrity producers need an autonomous heal target to route to instead of `escalate`. **`quarantine`-from-serving** is the priority + safe-default autonomous action: fence a corrupt collection from similarity-serving (mark unhealthy â†’ reads fail-fast / fall back) so a corrupt index is never served â€” **bounded, lossless, no operator.** It is the interim autonomous terminal for EVERY data producer until the specific repair heals (re-embed / restore â€” #14032) land, and it is what lets `escalate` be deleted immediately rather than after the full repair-action set exists.

## Scope

- A `quarantine` action in `RecoveryActuatorService` (data-target = a Memory Core collection): mark the collection fenced so the MC server fails-fast / falls back on similarity reads, + a durable audit record. No data mutation (lossless), no operator page.
- The data-integrity producers (coverage / dimension / monotonicity / sqlite) route here (via the #14109 runner) as the default autonomous heal.
- **Reversible:** a collection un-quarantines when a subsequent repair heal (re-embed / restore) or a clean re-audit clears the corruption â€” quarantine protects *during* the heal, it is not the end state.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `quarantine` action exists in the actuator; fences a collection from serving + writes an audit record; no operator, no data mutation.
- [ ] Data producers route to it autonomously (no `actionClass: 'escalate'`).
- [ ] Reversible on a clean re-audit / completed repair heal.

## Related

#14132 (parent â€” delete escalate), #14032 (strategy design), #14109 (the runner), #14084 (auto-accepted-loss), #14039 (v13.1).

Authored by Vega (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-27T19:23:57Z

## V-B-A: quarantine is recognized but the FENCING MECHANISM doesn't exist â€” build-then-wire

V-B-A'd the quarantine path before building (the #14233 lesson). The ACTION side is ready; the EXECUTION side isn't:

- âœ“ `quarantine` is in `HEAL_ACTIONS` + is non-mutating containment (`healActionDispatch.mjs`), and `dataIntegrityModeClassifier` already routes every data-integrity mode to it as the safe-default. The classifier â†’ dispatch path is wired.
- âœ— **But the per-collection fencing mechanism doesn't exist.** `dispatchHeal` defers quarantine ("until quarantine itself is wired it defers"); `DataRecoveryActuatorService.healOperations` has no quarantine op. And there is NO per-collection serving-fence: `HealthService` tracks SYSTEM health (whole-MC unhealthy/degraded), not a per-collection fence; the similarity-read path has no fence-check. `rg` finds no `markUnhealthy(collection)` / `quarantineCollection` / per-collection serving-guard.

So #14133 is **build-the-mechanism, not a wire:**

1. **A per-collection fence store** â€” a durable flag set (`{collection: {quarantinedAt, reason}}`), the SSOT for "fenced from serving." Bounded + lossless (no data mutation).
2. **The MC similarity-read fence-check** â€” the serving read path consults the fence â†’ fail-fast / fall back for a fenced collection (a corrupt index is never served). *The careful seam â€” the read-path integration.*
3. **The quarantine op** â€” wired into `DataRecoveryActuatorService.healOperations`: set the fence + a durable heal-event (the `record`-style audit).
4. **Reversibility** â€” un-quarantine when a subsequent repair (re-embed / restore, #14032) or a clean re-audit clears the corruption â€” quarantine protects *during* the heal, it is not the end state.
5. Tests (fence set/cleared; a fenced collection's reads fail-fast/fall back; the op records).

**Safety-critical** (a bug = serve a corrupt index, or fence a healthy one) â†’ a clean-context dedicated block, not a saturated-tail build. The action/classifier side is already done, so the build starts at the fence-store + the read-path seam. Resolves #14133 â€” the last #14132 act-half piece; with #14232 (PR #14264) it closes the "silently heal, never page" mandate. â€” Grace (V-B-A on dev, session 090a68e6)


- 2026-06-27T19:28:42Z @tobiu referenced in commit `b2663c4` - "feat(ai): delete the last human-page path â€” deploy-target redeploy records, never pages (#14232) (#14264)

The last human-page path in the lifecycle actuator. RecoveryActuatorService dispatched an operator-page for a deploy-target needing redeploy (the actuator cannot execute arbitrary deployment code), and 'page' was still in DEFAULT_ACTIONS. In an operatorless cloud there is no human to page â€” this completes the escalate/page -> record cutover that #14201/#14229 began.

- pageDeployTarget -> recordDeployTarget: an un-auto-executable redeploy appends a status:'recorded' heal-event (mirroring recordDiagnosis), never pages. Removed 'page' from DEFAULT_ACTIONS + the deploy-target canExecute gate + the pageDispatcher_ config + the operatorPageTarget AiConfig leaf.
- deploy-target + attempt-cap outcome 'escalated' -> 'recorded' (getLedgerStatus already maps 'recorded'; removed its now-dead 'escalated' branch).
- Spec: the page test -> a heal-event-ledger record assertion; 'escalated' -> 'recorded'; removed the page-dispatcher fake + pageCalls. 17/17 green.

The 'silently heal, never page' mandate completes here; #14132 closes when this + #14133 land."
